Reports.subject: Curriculum Committee Agenda Report
Reports.description: I've attached some screen shots of the report as it is running right now (which is indicating a Server Error) and some updates to the formatting and fields.  Most important for this report is the ability to identify the type of change that occurred in the proposal, i.e. prerequisites, MLOs, credit hours, Course Description, etc., which is already identified via the Comparison report. Can this be added on this report ("Impact" field)?

Reports.subject: NMAT 210 - Unit and Hours / Validation and Missing Data
Reports.description:
**This ticket created by NancyH on behalf of Craig Kutil**
* * *
A recent ticket added the CB22 field to the units and hours tab in course proposals.   The new CB22 field is not filtering correctly based on the credit status of N-Non Credit.
- See the image below you can see that the CB22 option of "Y" is not correct since it is a Non Credit Course.  According to the course detail in V2 this course should have "C" as selected value for CB22
- Also note that there are "0" hours in the total noncredit hours  / According to the same course in V2 it should have 72.0 Lecture hours and 72.0 Total Non Credit Hours.
- This course is part of program #565 - Foundational Mathematics Pathway - Certificate of Competency (Active).   The hours for for both the courses in the Program Course Block (NMAT 207 and NMAT 210) are showing as "0".  And the hours for NMAT 210 is also "0" but is also been set to override?
When I open the programs for the Course and Program
- Program Pathway - Shows the Label "Units" instead of Hours (This label was updated in a previous ticket.  Since it is noncredit the REPORT should show "HOURS" instead of UNITS
- Previously when I ran the Program Requirements Report the  Label is CORRECT (Hours).  However, the NMAT 207 shows 108.0 instead of "0" that was shown in the pathway report.  (maybe the data was changed since I first ran the report - but we need to look and make sure that something is not updating in the background and changing the value int he report.
- Program Narrative - Showing the correct Label (Hours).  NMAT 207 is reporting the same as in the Program Requirements noted just above.
- Course outline report - shows "0" Hours in Meta.  In V2 the course outline is reporting "72"hours
What to do:
- Check the Courses and ensure that the hours are being calculated correctly.
- Check and correct the CB Validation so the correct options appear in the CB22 dropdown
- Check to make sure that the data from V2 was imported into this course
- Check to make sure that the hours are being reflected correctly in the program - course blocks and in all reports (course and program)
- When the discrepancies are identified, run a query to identify all course/programs that have the same conditions so they can all be identified and corrected.
 ![](https://governet.zendesk.com/attachments/token/1TWCIzMurtfdMteYYVceRJrlJ/?name=image.png)
 ![](https://governet.zendesk.com/attachments/token/KifGBDoJoNSaLDvNle7C6Ript/?name=image.png)
 ![](https://governet.zendesk.com/attachments/token/0bLpKmtAsIxFwzdHJMMOLIf3j/?name=image.png)

Reports.subject: SPC - Transfer Plan
Reports.description:
Can you find a few minutes today to give me a call please? I was going to wait until our call tomorrow, but Angela asked me to reach out today.
I’m not sure exactly what the problem is nor even how to explain it easily, especially in writing.  I’d rather talk/walk you through it first.  Then figure out next steps.
Starting with the report from # 37226 (Historical courses), there are 112 programs listed, and the majority are transfer plans.  When looking at a lot of those transfer plans they have many historical courses.  I don’t know why.
For example…FTLIT-TR (id 1145), in the course block under the AA Gen Ed block, you will see many historical versions of the courses.  And, when you open the Program of Study report and click on the course link, the course description is from the historical version of the course…not the most current/active version.  I followed POS 2041 and this historical version is 2 proposal ago
 ![](https://governet.zendesk.com/attachments/token/8kaVueV7wlrHk1f3lEv3VMi8y/?name=inlineImage.png)
I looked in V2 at the same program (id 1389) at the course blocks, but can’t tell there which version of the course it is using.
 ![](https://governet.zendesk.com/attachments/token/yxWTiVxvHKbmFlWpLGYwFCqpf/?name=inlineImage.png)
So I looked at the Look Up Data table for General Education Requirements for the Social Sciences
 ![](https://governet.zendesk.com/attachments/token/8BVgPMrGSA30w2paF6ch7TzOl/?name=inlineImage.png)
 ![](https://governet.zendesk.com/attachments/token/BHiXd2XaPdQY9M62hPmZBOQ2F/?name=inlineImage.png)
When selecting the courses button, it doesn’t see like I can edit (which is probably now correct since V2 is basically inactive).
When you look at the Program of Study, the courses no longer have links, so can’t check there either for the correct course version.
**So, the question is, how do we fix all the historical courses in the course blocks in Meta?  We have about 89 TRs and all will need to be reviewed.** Granted, the caveat is that some of the courses may truly be historical and there is not active version.  I haven’t had a chance to really dig into this, but also wanted to get with you before spending too much time before we know what is going on.
Check the Course Block library, that might be the issue. AA Gen ED requirements.

Reports.subject: COURSES Reports - Course Impact Programs fix (from CS188 / ZD 39053)
Reports.description:
This ticket relates to ticket # 39053. It was not resolved correctly.
The COURSE IMPACT report (#419), in the PROGRAMS section should be:
Program Title
(Program Code)
“Status”
Effective Term
And include ALL program statuses:
DRAFT
All APPROVAL workflow statuses
ACTIVE
HISTORICAL
where Effective Term ≤ 5 yrs
([term – 75 = past term --- rolling terms]– EX: 640 through present; 635 through 560, 630 through 555, 625 through 550, etc.)
List of Programs should be listed in alphabetical order by PROGRAM CODE,
then by Effective Term newest to oldest
(will typically be draft/approval workflow status, then active, then historicals).
PROGRAMS refers to all program types:  Programs, Transfer Plans, and Articulations.
ACG 2071 (9738) Course Impact report (419) should look something like this:
Accounting Technology Operations (ACTAP-CT) Active (640)
Accounting Technology Operations (ACTAP-CT) Historical (625)
Business Administration (BUS-AS) Active (640)
Business Administration (BUS-AS) Historical (625)
Business Administration (BUS-AS) Historical (610)
Business Administration (BUS-AS) Historical (585)
Business Administration (BUS-AS) Historical (625)
Business Administration (BUS-BS) Active (610)
Business Administration (BUS-BS) Historical (625)
Business Administration (BUS-TR) Active (640)
Business Administration (BUS-TR) Historical (610)
Business Administration (BUS-TR) Historical (580)
FAMU Accounting (FMACC-TR) Active (640)
FAMU Accounting (FMACC-TR) Historical (610)
FAMU Accounting (FMACC-TR) Historical (580)
etc…
(see attachment to see the way the report looks today)
Let me know if any questions.
Shirley

Reports.subject: SPC - REPORTS - Program of Study & Comparison - Program - Follow up - #43221
Reports.description:
I would like to request that all our tickets’ status be set to “Waiting on your reply” before deploying to production.  We should have a chance to test/review the work done before deployment.  Otherwise, we will have to ask to have everything posted to the sandbox first.
This ticket is (again) a perfect example.
The text added is correct and in the right place. However, and this piece is on us, where the effective term is noted, the text (Fall 2022 (610)) should be the same effective term of that specific program.  The verbiage is static; the effective term is dynamic (and is the same term as that specific program).
  ![](https://acadea.zendesk.com/attachments/token/JMiZ0w8WddPvpScfqX5LcF8SE/?name=inlineImage.jpeg) ![](https://acadea.zendesk.com/attachments/token/4zr53gMEWMzEQAlXMstqfEdyc/?name=inlineImage.jpeg)
 ![](https://acadea.zendesk.com/attachments/token/gpJrjnl96sjwZRoqYzbayyAQC/?name=inlineImage.jpeg)
We should have been able to test/review this ticket before put to productuon.  We would have caught this before deployment.  Now we need this fixed ASAP/URGENT as these reports  are student/all users-facing.  Students start classes on Monday so it is imperative it is fixed …like, yesterday.
I’m OK with it being fixed on production as long as there is someone (you or someone) on your end to double check (QA) this.  (If it can’t be fixed today, then at least put it back the way it was and then have someone fix it on Monday.)
Please let me know how soon this can be completed, and how this process will work going forward.
Also let me know that you have received this and if any questions.

Courses.subject: SPC - URGENT  Course requisite and level 500 errors etc. Credentials
Courses.description:
**SEE ATTACHED PDF FOR FULL DETAILS IN THE EMAIL THREAD.**
Angela and Troy,
I think we have a multi-level issue going on here.  Please note that I’ve received the same 500-level errors when stepping thru the code, but everything from this point in the email is a reference to Postman.
LIVE: https://mws.services.curriqunet.com/scripts/webservices/generic_meta/clients/versions/v5/stpetersburg.cfc?method=getEntityData
TEST: https://mwsdev.services.curriqunet.com/scripts/webservices/generic_meta/clients/versions/v5/stpetersburg.cfc?method=getEntityData
Username: stevens.paul@spcollege.edu
MAC2311
LIVE: 500 - An internal error occcured, please reference RequestId 8000300c-0001-df00-b63f-84710c7967bb when contacting support.
TEST: 200 - Okay
DEP2004
LIVE: 500 - An internal error occcured, please reference RequestId 8000300c-0001-df00-b63f-84710c7967bb when contacting support.
TEST: 500 - An internal error occurred -- RequestId: 8000b248-0007-fb00-b63f-84710c7967bb; Exception message: Expected to find column AACodeId in entity data for table CourseAACode, but did not.
DEP2102
LIVE: 500 - An internal error occcured, please reference RequestId 8000300c-0001-df00-b63f-84710c7967bb when contacting support.
TEST: 500 - An internal error occurred -- RequestId: 80026be5-0007-e600-b63f-84710c7967bb; Exception message: Expected to find column AACodeId in entity data for table CourseAACode, but did not.
CLP2140
LIVE: 500 - An internal error occcured, please reference RequestId 8000300c-0001-df00-b63f-84710c7967bb when contacting support.
TEST: 500 - An internal error occurred -- RequestId: 8002ec95-0001-db00-b63f-84710c7967bb; Exception message: Expected to find column AACodeId in entity data for table CourseAACode, but did not.
And so on…
Based on past issues, I would bet that the admin access associated with my credentials has been reset again (the CN team can confirm whether or not that’s the case).  The GUID return codes mean nothing to us, but the type of error message we’re seeing associated with them in the test environment suggest an issue with at the CN database level (which we have no control over).
If there’s an escalation path we can follow, that would be great.  If it’s as simple a problem as I think it is (credentials), we should be able to wrap this today.
Thanks,
Paul

Courses.subject: SPC - Approval Workflows
Courses.description:
Hi Natalie…
**HIGH IMPORTANCE**
We are having some issues with the Courses Approval  Workflows. We have a C&I Committee meeting next Friday to the members in their part of this process and we are running into snags. Please review the info below ASAP.  **Would like fixed no later then end of day Wednesday.** We will need Thursday to do color group assignments and make any other adjustments.
Angela is available for a phone call this afternoon until 5:00 (or Monday morning is your office is working on Monday) my time for any questions or need clarity on the info below.
Approval Workflows
11 Courses have been approved at level 2 Tech Review and are in Level 3 and waiting C&I Color group assignment. However, only 2 courses have been assigned a color group.
 ![](https://governet.zendesk.com/attachments/token/VpF25Ztm4Pjf6nVb0mGvABsdt/?name=inlineImage.jpeg)
ATE 2612 has been assigned the Blue group
 ![](https://governet.zendesk.com/attachments/token/z8wTdjOnYiMdUSeALx5m0ZYQj/?name=inlineImage.png)
PHI 2642 is assigned to the Green Group
Logged in as a C&I member of the Red Group
candi@spcollege.edu
changeme1
All the courses sitting at the level above is showing the Position of C&I Red Group.  Why is this?
Shouldn’t this be blank until a color group has actually been assigned?
The 2 courses that have a different color group (noted above) are showing as Red Group.  Why is this?
The C&I Committee member should only see courses assigned to their specific group (unless course set to all).  And they should not see them in Approvals until that color has been assigned to the course.
So for the user above, Candi should not be seeing any courses/approvals as none are assigned to her/red.
**Shirley Boyette**
_Research/Curriculum Analyst_
Curriculum Services | EPI Tech
727.341.3041 | boyette.shirley@spcollege.edu

Other.subject: Assistance for API question
Other.description:
We are working on our API and trying to test but have these questions:
Do we have a new or different production endpoint (or endpoints) for making API calls?
We were using this endpoint originally to test:
https://securedev.curricunet.com/scripts/webservices/generic_meta/clients/versions/v5/
We were advised that the response time would be better if we used THIS endpoint:
https://mwsdev.services.curriqunet.com/scripts/webservices/generic_meta/clients/versions/v5/
Provided documentation suggests this is the production endpoint:
https://secure.curriqunet.com/scripts/webservices/generic_meta/clients/versions/v5/
But that’s not working.  Is there some other endpoint we need to use for production?

Occasionally the script will execute, but no results are returned.

Programs.subject: St. Petersburg Web services
Programs.description:
[image: .]
Hello Bill,
The St. Petersburg project has been moved over to our Maintenance
Department. Travis is the manager of that Department and I have included
him on this email. Please work with him to make any additional changes or
updates to the webservices.
Thanks,
[image: from]
Jared Pratt
*Development Manager*
*Governet*
1000 River Walk Drive, Suite 350
Idaho Falls, ID 83402
208.522.1225 | Office
208.522.2896 | Fax
jpratt@governet.net
http://www.governet.net/
*Building the Worldwide Curriculum Network  *
*From:* Bill Bliss [mailto:Bliss.Bill@spcollege.edu]
*Sent:* Friday, May 24, 2013 1:39 PM
*To:* Jared Pratt; Timothy McDowell
*Cc:* Margaret Bowman; Angela Ashe
*Subject:* RE: St. Petersburg CurricUNET move to Maintenance/Support
Hello Jared,
I’ve started looking at the web service functions provided.  I'm sorry to
say they are not working as expected.  I have not gone through all of them,
but the first 2 I tried were not working, so I stopped.
The ones I tried are the "GET_PROGRAM_COURSE_BLOCK" function, which seems
to be returning mostly the "GET_PROGRAM_DATA" information (but not all of
it), and the "GET_PROGRAM_DATA" function, which returns an error:* Function
("GET_PROGRAM_DATA") is not a valid method for this service*.  Looking at
the WSDL confirms that.
The way all of these functions have been constructed seems rather
restrictive, especially on how we can pull filtered information.  For
example, let us suppose there are several degree programs offered by the
“College of Business” (CoB).   The program information contains the
“College of Business” in the COLLEGE_SCHOOL_DEPARTMENT field – however,
that field is not one I can use to filter requests.  If I wanted to
generate a list of those programs, I would have to get a list of all
programs, then manually parse out the ones associated with the  CoB.  It
would certainly be easier for me if I could define my parameters in an
array to be passed in the SOAP call.  I realize this would require
completely rewriting what you’ve done – but I hope you will consider it for
future modifications.  Until then…
For the Course functions, please re-order the arguments to:
Prefix
Course_number
Status
Curricunet_id
and if the Status argument could default to whatever value represents
Active, that would be great. Then we only need to specify the prefix and
course number – which is our natural way of searching for courses.
Since “curricunet_id” is an identifier that is internal to your product,
I’m not sure how we would ever use it in our applications.  It would be
fine with me if you could just eliminate it – but moving it to the end
effectively makes it ignorable, so that would work, too.  This applies to
the program functions, too.
A service that gives us access to just the basic course info would be
helpful – something that returns the course title, number of credits, and
description when given the course prefix and (optionally) course_number
would be ideal.
Here are some examples of how we use the course data from our existing
system:
·         To generate a list of all courses where the prefix begins with a
specific letter: http://www.spcollege.edu/courses/prefix/A
·         To generate a list of all courses with a specific prefix:
http://www.spcollege.edu/courses/prefix/ACG
·         To list a specific course:
http://www.spcollege.edu/popcourse/acg-2021
As I’ve mentioned, my goal is to use the data from CurricuNet to display on
our public web site with similar functionality as our current system.
Another example of how we would use this data, this one a program
description output, is http://www.spcollege.edu/courses/program/BUS-BS.  It
was recently pointed out to me that your system can generate something
similar.  If you could provide us with a simple service whereby we can get
that output – a basic program description similar to what we already have –
that would be excellent.  It looks like (once the program functions are
working) we could build that kind of an output, but it would certainly be
simpler if we could just request the description for “BUS-BS” and then just
worry about handling the display.  Since your product already generates
that kind of output, I hope it won’t be too difficult to incorporate into
these functions.
Thanks for your time and effort.
Bill Bliss
Manager, Web Programming & Support
Web & Instructional Tech.
St. Petersburg College
Phone: 727.394.6226
*From:* Timothy McDowell
[mailto:tmcdowell@governet.net ]
*Sent:* Wednesday, April 03, 2013 10:39 AM
*To:* Bill Bliss; Jared Pratt; Leigh Goldberg Hopf; Margaret Bowman; Angela
Ashe
*Cc:* Travis Hillyard; Todd Andersen
*Subject:* RE: St. Petersburg CurricUNET move to Maintenance/Support
[image: From]
[image: Governet]
[image: .]
Good morning, Bill,
Jared’s team has worked on completing an updated web service for St.
Petersburg. Please see the attached document and direct further questions
to Jared.
Thank you,
[image: clip_image003]
Timothy McDowell
*Project Manager/Training and Development Administrator*
*Governet*
1000 River Walk Drive, Suite 350
Idaho Falls, ID 83402
208.522.1225 | Office
208.522.2896 | Fax
tmcdowell@governet.net
http://www.governet.net/
*Building the Worldwide Curriculum Network  *
*From:* Bill Bliss [mailto:Bliss.Bill@spcollege.edu]
*Sent:* Wednesday, April 03, 2013 5:38 AM
*To:* Jared Pratt; Leigh Goldberg Hopf; Margaret Bowman; Angela Ashe
*Cc:* Travis Hillyard; Todd Andersen; Timothy McDowell
*Subject:* Re: St. Petersburg CurricUNET move to Maintenance/Support
Jared,
In early January, I was exchanging emails with you regarding web services
(see below).  Since 1/16, I have not received any further information – let
alone complete documentation on accessing the CurricuNet web services.  I
believe Leigh also followed up on this in March.
Is that phase still in development, or should I now work with your support
team to implement access to the data?
Thanks,
Bill Bliss
Manager, Web Programming & Support
Web & Instructional Tech.
St. Petersburg College
Phone: 727.394.6226
*From:* Bill Bliss
*Sent:* Wednesday, January 16, 2013 10:16 AM
*To:* 'Jared Pratt'
*Cc:* Leigh Goldberg Hopf
*Subject:* RE: Follow-up to conference call about web services
Hello Jared,
This is much more helpful than the WSDL file – thanks.  The concise
descriptions of the parameters and their expected values/formats is just
what we need.
When you send the next iteration, rather than inserting a screen-shot of
the XML returned (which is very difficult for me to read), a description of
the recordset would be more helpful.  To use the “getCourses” example, it
would be helpful to have something along the lines of:
The output for getCourses contains:
Count_Retake_Credit (“Yes” or “No”)
Count_Retake_Times (integer)
Courses_ID (Curricunet internal ID: integer)
Course_Number (Institutional course number, without prefix, e.g. 1101 or
1101H)
Course_Status (“Active” “Pending” or “Historical”)
And so forth…
Finally – a brief description of non-obvious expected behavior for each
method would be helpful.  For example, in the getCourses method, all the
parameters are marked as optional.  The obvious expected behavior is that,
if I enter only a single “Courses_ID” value, I will get that one record.
Likewise if I enter only a “STATUS” value, I will get all records with that
status.  But what happens when I set no parameters?  That is what I mean by
non-obvious.  Do I get every single record, or do I get none?
This part is, I admit, a little picky – but it’s one of my personal
peeves:  It would be nice if the method and parameter names were
consistent.  For example, make the naming convention for methods either
camel case (“getCourses”) or underscored (“get_requisites”) – not both.
Likewise, decide whether parameters should be all caps (as with getCourses)
or all lower-case (as with all the others).
I look forward to seeing the next round of documentation.
Bill Bliss
Manager, Web Programming & Support
Web & Instructional Tech.
St. Petersburg College
Phone: 727.394.6226
*From:* Jared Pratt [mailto:jpratt@governet.net  ]
*Sent:* Tuesday, January 15, 2013 11:37 AM
*To:* Bill Bliss
*Cc:* Leigh Goldberg Hopf
*Subject:* RE: Follow-up to conference call about web services
[image: .]
Attached is some additional documentation on our default web service. These
web services can be configured with some additional methods and parameters
to meet your needs.
Thanks,
[image: from]
Jared Pratt
*Development Manager*
*Governet*
1000 River Walk Drive, Suite 350
Idaho Falls, ID 83402
208.522.1225 | Office
208.522.2896 | Fax
jpratt@governet.net
http://www.governet.net/
*Building the Worldwide Curriculum Network  *
*From:* Bill Bliss [mailto:Bliss.Bill@spcollege.edu]
*Sent:* Thursday, January 10, 2013 6:14 AM
*To:* Jared Pratt (jpratt@governet.net)
*Cc:* Leigh Goldberg Hopf
*Subject:* RE: Follow-up to conference call about web services
Hello Jared,
Thank you for sending the WSDL file for your web services.  Unfortunately,
you didn’t include any documentation detailing the functionality and
utilization.  While it is helpful to know, for example, that the parameters
COURSES_ID, STATUS, DISCIPLINE, and COURSE_NUMBER are needed to access the
operation “getCourses” – we have no idea what those parameters should
contain (other than a string), or in what format.  To me, a valid
COURSES_ID is ‘ENC-1101’ – but I don’t have any way of knowing from the
WSDL file what your service is expecting.
Likewise, we have no idea what to expect as returns for the various
operations.  We could certainly do a test on each one, but it would be much
less time consuming if you could provide that information.
Also, I’m sure there is some kind of authentication required to access this
data, but I don’t see that in your email, and I’m not seeing any indication
of it in the WSDL file.
Thank you,
Bill Bliss
Manager, Web Programming & Support
Web & Instructional Tech.
St. Petersburg College
Phone: 727.394.6226
*From:* Leigh Goldberg Hopf
*Sent:* Thursday, January 10, 2013 7:43 AM
*To:* Bill Bliss
*Cc:* Jared Pratt (jpratt@governet.net)
*Subject:* FW: Follow-up to conference call about web services
Bill,
Attached you will find the web services info that Jared has provided—as a
follow-up to our call in December.  I have copied him on this email too—in
case there are additional questions that you have, after your review.
Give me a call, or email, and we will find a time to chat.
Thanks,
Leigh
Leigh Hopf
Director, Institutional Research
St. Petersburg College
Phone: 727.341.3383

[image: .]
Hello,
I have attached the typical WSDL. I did modify it and replace the previous
college information with “stpetersburg” so you can see that this is a
typical webservice call. This WSDL shows you what methods and parameters
you have to get the data that you need. We can customize this to have
multiple method calls and additional prameters if needed. Your IT staff
will need to create an interface that consumes this service, calls the
necessary methods to extract the data that you need. The data is extracted
into xml format which they should be able to do about anything with.
I hope this helps. Once we are finished loading your data we can get a
basic webservice put in place for you to test this.
Thanks,

Programs.subject: SPC - Workflows and Sandbox - UYRGENT - DUE 01/19
Programs.description:
In looking at both the workflows and C&I color groups we are still experiencing problems. I have not had a chance to check everything against the ticket and what was talked about this morning, but below is what I have checked. We’ll need everything completed from Zendesk ticket 36577 and everything that was talked about this morning. Our C&I Committee meets tomorrow morning, so we are in a time crunch for being able to have this all ready and working for them. Once these are all fixed in production we will need another refresh to the sandbox, please.
1. **Workflows **– please see the screenshots in the email trail below.
1. **C&I Color Groups** – Tested in sandbox
- candi@spcollege.edu was assigned C&I Yellow Group
- I changed ATE 2612 from blue assignment to yellow, this was the only course assigned to yellow but all these other courses still come up as well.
- Also, it seems to only be pulling in certain courses due to workflow issues and not pulling everything that has gone through our Level 2 Technical Review.
  - E.g., CJE 2645 (was approved in prod tech review 1/10/23), DEH 2804L (was approved in prod tech review 1/12/2023)
![](https://governet.zendesk.com/attachments/token/XbhCb4LvWeGxnPfFqANI8mQzs/?name=inlineImage.png)
- I then tested the disapproval on ATE 2612 and it did the same as before, changing all the other member actions to “Waiting on Changes” which makes the proposal disappear for all others but doesn’t move it. It also adds the Pending Originator Changes text at the top and marks the originator’s action (Natavia Middleton) as “Pending.”
![](https://governet.zendesk.com/attachments/token/MANfqJa1EWKudi5sXhqLZrE11/?name=inlineImage.png)
**Subject:** RE: Workflows and Sandbox
**WORKFLOWS**
I logged out and logged back in to check the workflows, haven’t looked deep into them but from a quick glance **Major Course Mods** still doesn’t show the update..
 ![](https://governet.zendesk.com/attachments/token/LBhntTAt3spfyJLhYw9wtRsqQ/?name=inlineImage.jpeg)
 ![](https://governet.zendesk.com/attachments/token/wJI4ACZBENW1bap8cgeLatFHr/?name=inlineImage.jpeg)
**Current Minor Course Mod** also looks older i.e., the names/positions.
 ![](https://governet.zendesk.com/attachments/token/kOZoQsiP3IVdpaKmFQrXQC2mZ/?name=inlineImage.jpeg)
**New Course** shows it was updated and the status is in there but the positions we had are back to saying “Curriculum Services” instead of “Technical Review #1, #2”, “BOT”, “Implementation” and Level titles didn’t pull in. Not a big deal as long as the flow and statuses are correct but I know for a fact Shirley and I worked on these together and made these changes in real time so not sure what’s going on in that sense.
 ![](https://governet.zendesk.com/attachments/token/3Jr3KGhPvo7bMWGliwwqUc3eM/?name=inlineImage.jpeg)
 ![](https://governet.zendesk.com/attachments/token/bcAPKQrcwk7bprx0MuueFwGGI/?name=inlineImage.jpeg)

System.subject: SPC - API - Documentation - URGENT
System.description:
I would be remiss if I didn’t express our frustration over this situation.  I believe that we have asked for documentation and updates with system changes such as this.  While I don’t know the exact protocol, I do know that this has happened several times and leads to a negative impact on us as well as our students.  The information that has been impacted with this most recent issue, is attached to a requirement of the state of Florida, putting us out of compliance.  I believe this can be avoided if we are given information on these type of changes prior to implementation and my understanding is that this is a standard process.
We need to create documentaion that shows the name of the fields and the name in the API and if we can store this in Meta and keep it updated when there is any changes, that would be the best. This has caused several issues at SPC and we need to get this resolved ASAP.
